mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer and business demand growth, which accelerates corporate revenue cycles and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a classification accuracy above 55%, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints, which trigger inflationary pressures and force equity repricing, yielding negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases correlates with positive 5-day SPX returns, or the predictive slope coefficient flips sign and becomes positive.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 3.0 percent impose high financing costs on leveraged firms and suppress risk appetite, causing discount rate compression to reverse and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in high-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero alpha over 20-day horizons, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal rising household wealth effects and construction sector activity, which propagate through supply chains and boost corporate earnings, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a hit rate above 52%, or the regression coefficient becomes negative and significant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding above 105 during post-shock recovery phases indicates successful capacity utilization and easing supply constraints, which reduces inflation fears and triggers positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO exceeds 105 during recovery regimes, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance.
